outcome_text: Processes and procedures are in place for decommissioning and phasing out AI systems safely and in a manner that does not increase risks or decrease the organization's trustworthiness.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI system decommissioning procedures, including safe phase-out planning, risk assessment during decommissioning, data handling and retention requirements, stakeholder communication, and maintaining organizational trustworthiness throughout the system retirement process.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI system decommissioning procedures, including safe phase-out planning, risk assessment during decommissioning, data handling and retention requirements, stakeholder communication, and maintaining organizational trustworthiness throughout the system retirement process.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI model lifecycle management, including model versioning, drift detection, performance monitoring, retraining triggers, and decommissioning criteria.
comments: NIST AI RMF GV-1.7 (GOVERN) equal WIDAI DG domain. DG-K-006 is the strongest match (STS: 0.660, cosine: 0.633). Secondary: AG-K-002 (AG, cosine: 0.571), AG-T-001 (AG, cosine: 0.566).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.6/10 (raw 0.660). Shared vocabulary: [ai, decommissioning, knowledge]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [assessment, communication, data, handling, maintaining, organizational] vs WIDAI terms [criteria, detection, drift, lifecycle, management, model] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.894 (P=0.884, R=0.904). Balanced P/R — the 30-word competency implication and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.4%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.633 ≈ STS 0.660 (gap 0.027). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, decommissioning, knowledge] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.4%. Shared tokens: [ai, decommissioning, knowledge]. NIST AI RMF-only: [assessment, communication, data, handling, maintaining, organizational]. WIDAI-only: [criteria, detection, drift, lifecycle, management, model].
